| socks2                = ffffff
| First game            = {{flagdeco|FRA|1794}} '''Madagascar''' 1–2 {{fb-rt|MRI|1923}} <br /> ([[Madagascar]]; Date Unknown 1947)
| Largest win           = {{fb|MAD}} 8–1 {{fb-rt|CGO}} <br /> ([[Antananarivo]], [[Madagascar]]; 19 April 1960)
| Largest loss          = {{nowrap|{{fb|MRI|1923}} 7–0 '''Madagascar''' {{flagdeco|FRA}} <br /> ([[Réunion]]; 31 July 1952) <br /> {{fb|DRC|1963|name=Congo-Léopoldville}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|MAD}} <br /> ([[Republic of the Congo|Congo]]; 18 July 1965)}}
| Regional name         = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps     = 1
| Regional cup first    = [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
| Regional cup best     = Quarter-finals ([[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]])
| 2ndRegional name      = [[African Nations Championship]] 
| 2ndRegional cup apps  = 2 
| 2ndRegional cup first = [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]] 
| 2ndRegional cup best  = Runners-up ([[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]) 
| 3rdRegional name      = [[COSAFA Cup]]
| 3rdRegional cup apps  = 12
| 3rdRegional cup first = [[2002 COSAFA Cup|2002]]
| 3rdRegional cup best  = Third place ([[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]])
| medaltemplates        = 
{{MedalCompetition|[[African Nations Championship]]}}
{{MedalSilver|[[2024 African Nations Championship|2024 Kenya, Tanzania and Uganda]]|}}
{{MedalBronze|[[2022 African Nations Championship|2022 Algeria]]|}}
{{MedalCompetition|[[COSAFA Cup]]}}
{{MedalBronze|[[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015 South Africa]]|}}
}}
The '''Madagascar national football team''', nicknamed '''''Barea''''' after the island's [[zebu]],<ref>{{Cite web |last=Clarel Faniry Rasoanaivo |date=10 July 2019 |title=Madagascans elated by AFCON success, eye semi-finals |url=https://www.reuters.com/article/soccer-nations-madagascar/madagascans-elated-by-afcon-success-eye-semi-finals-idUKKCN1U52G9 |access-date=10 July 2019 |website=[[Reuters]] |archive-date=10 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190710230217/https://www.reuters.com/article/soccer-nations-madagascar/madagascans-elated-by-afcon-success-eye-semi-finals-idUKKCN1U52G9 |url-status=live }}</ref> is the national team of [[Madagascar]] and is controlled by the [[Malagasy Football Federation]]. It has never qualified for the finals of the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]]. It took part in its first [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]. Among its biggest wins was a 1–0 home victory over [[Egypt national football team|Egypt]] in the [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|qualification rounds]] of the [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations]] until being surpassed by a recent 2–0 win over [[Nigeria national football team|Nigeria]] in the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations Group B|group stage]] of the 2019 Africa Cup of Nations. Reflecting the official name of the country at the time, the team was known as the '''Malagasy Republic national football team''' between 1958 and 1975.

==History==
Madagascar made its debut in 1947 in a home match against [[Mauritius national football team|Mauritius]] which was lost 2–1. The game was part of a ''Triangulaire'' tournament between Madagascar, Mauritius and [[Réunion national football team|Reunion]]. Madagascar's second match was at home to Reunion and saw their first ever win, 4–2. The triangular tournament was staged every year until 1958, and Madagascar's highest-scoring game was on 13 July 1953 when they beat Reunion 6–4 at home. Their worst loss was on 31 July 1952 when they lost 7–0 to Mauritius in Reunion.

Madagascar played against opposition different from Mauritius or Reunion for the first time in 1960, as part of a tournament between French-speaking nations held on the island. In their first match on 15 April 1960 they beat the Upper Volta (now [[Burkina Faso national football team|Burkina Faso]]) 6–1. The second game was a quarter-final against [[France national football team|France]]'s amateur side on 17 April and was lost 2–1. On 19 April Madagascar beat the [[Congo national football team|Congo]] 8–1 in the third-place play-off.

In April 1963, Madagascar entered another competition for French-speaking countries, this time in [[Senegal]], and was placed in a group with Dahomey (now [[Benin national football team|Benin]]), [[Liberia national football team|Liberia]] and [[Chad national football team|Chad]]. They opened with a 1–0 win over Dahomey on 11 April, and then beat Chad 2–1 on 13 April and Liberia 3–1 on 15 April. In the semi-finals, Madagascar were beaten 2–1 by [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]] on 19 April, and then lost 4–1 to France's amateur team in the third-place play-off on 21 April.

In September 1963 the ''Triangulaire'' between Madagascar, Reunion and Mauritius was reinstated for the first time since 1958. On 15 September they beat Reunion 6–1 at home, and on 18 September drew 1–1 to Mauritius in a home game which was abandoned.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Courtney |first=Barrie |title=Madagascar&nbsp;– List of International Matches |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/madag-intres.html |access-date=2 April 2021 |publisher=Rec.Sport.Soccer Statistics Foundation |archive-date=23 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230423172403/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesm/madag-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

On 16 October 2018, Madagascar qualified to the [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations]] for the first time in their history, after they won 1–0 against [[Equatorial Guinea national football team|Equatorial Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=16 October 2018 |title=Africa Cup of Nations: Egypt, Tunisia, Senegal, Madagascar qualify for the finals |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/45878968 |website=BBC |access-date=19 October 2018 |archive-date=19 October 2018 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20181019130339/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/45878968 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=16 October 2018 |title=Madagascar reaches first African Cup, Egypt also qualifies |url=https://www.washingtonpost.com/sports/dcunited/madagascar-1st-team-to-qualify-for-african-cup-of-nations/2018/10/16/bf163d02-d161-11e8-a4db-184311d27129_story.html?noredirect=on |newspaper=The Washington Post}}{{dead link|date=June 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref> During their debut match against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] on 22 June 2019,<ref>{{cite web| url = https://www.cafonline.com/total-africa-cup-of-nations/teams/21/| title = CAFOnline.com| access-date = 24 June 2019| archive-date = 24 June 2019| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20190624213342/https://www.cafonline.com/total-africa-cup-of-nations/teams/21/| url-status = live}}</ref> [[Anicet Abel]] scored Madagascar's first-ever AFCON goal and secured their first point of the competition with a 2–2 draw against Guinea.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Business & Financial News, U.S & International Breaking News &#124; Reuters |url=https://www.reuters.com/?edition-redirect=af |access-date=2 April 2021 |website=www.reuters.com |archive-date=13 August 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190813044126/https://af.reuters.com/article/egyptNews/idAFLDE70Q1Y520110127 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Madagascar earn draw in Afcon debut |work=BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/48645248 |access-date=2 April 2021 |archive-date=8 November 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20201108002141/https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/football/48645248 |url-status=live }}</ref> The team defeated [[Burundi national football team|Burundi]] in their second match<ref>{{Cite web |title=Afcon 2019: Burundi won't lose hope after Madagascar defeat, says coach Olivier Niyungeko &#124; Goal.com |url=https://www.goal.com/en/news/afcon-2019-burundi-wont-lose-hope-after-madagascar-defeat/1e6nn40wwv0m51t9h206inrgjb |access-date=2 April 2021 |website=www.goal.com |archive-date=18 August 2020 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20200818102056/https://www.goal.com/en/news/afcon-2019-burundi-wont-lose-hope-after-madagascar-defeat/1e6nn40wwv0m51t9h206inrgjb |url-status=live }}</ref> and followed it up with a 2–0 victory over the [[Super Eagles]] of Nigeria to top Group B.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Madagascar Stun Nigeria, 2-0, As Debutantes Win AFCON Group B |url=https://www.beinsports.com/us/afcon/video/madagascar-stun-nigeria-2-0-as-debutante-bare/1230711 |access-date=2 April 2021 |website=beIN SPORTS USA |archive-date=1 March 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210301141500/https://www.beinsports.com/us/afcon/video/madagascar-stun-nigeria-2-0-as-debutante-bare/1230711 |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Africa Cup of Nations 2019: The surprise package from Madagascar |date=1 July 2019 |url=https://www.dw.com/en/africa-cup-of-nations-2019-the-surprise-package-from-madagascar/a-49432873 |access-date=2 April 2021 |publisher=Deutsche Welle |archive-date=14 February 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210214111618/https://www.dw.com/en/africa-cup-of-nations-2019-the-surprise-package-from-madagascar/a-49432873 |url-status=live }}</ref> Then Madagascar advanced to quarter-finals after beating DR Congo in the round of 16.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Africa Cup of Nations 2019: Madagascar stun DR Congo, Algeria ease through |date=7 July 2019 |url=https://www.dw.com/en/africa-cup-of-nations-2019-madagascar-stun-dr-congo-algeria-ease-through/a-49505887 |access-date=2 April 2021 |publisher=Deutsche Welle |archive-date=2 November 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20211102131508/https://www.dw.com/en/africa-cup-of-nations-2019-madagascar-stun-dr-congo-algeria-ease-through/a-49505887 |url-status=live }}</ref> The team's successful performance was coined by pundits as the [[Iceland national football team|Iceland]] of Africa, resembling the shockingly successful debut of Iceland in [[UEFA Euro 2016]].<ref>{{Cite web |last=Sharuko |first=Robson |date=10 July 2019 |title=Africa: Madagascar's French Armada Reminds Warriors of the British Brigade We Missed |url=https://allafrica.com/stories/201907100768.html |access-date=2 April 2021 |website=allAfrica.com |archive-date=11 July 2019 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190711191502/https://allafrica.com/stories/201907100768.html |url-status=live }}</ref> However, Madagascar's dream ended abruptly after suffering a 0–3 defeat at the hand of another former champion, [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=11 July 2019 |title=Madagascar 0-3 Tunisia: As it happened |url=https://www.thesouthafrican.com/sport/soccer/afcon-2019-live-madagascar-vs-tunisia-quarter-final/ |access-date=2 April 2021 |archive-date=23 June 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210623175828/https://www.thesouthafrican.com/sport/soccer/afcon-2019-live-madagascar-vs-tunisia-quarter-final/ |url-status=live }}</ref>

==Results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Corentin Martins]]
|-
|Assistant coaches 
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Andry Razakandrianaina<br>{{flagicon|MAD}} Hervé Andrianasimbola
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Nirina Rakotobololona
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Michel Rajaonarinantenaina
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Flavien Andriamanomenjanahary
|-
|Performance coach
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Alphonse Rakotomanatsinoro
|-
|Team doctors
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Dr. Stéphane Rajaonaribololona<br>{{flagicon|MAD}} Dr. Hugo Rakotomalalanantenaina
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|MAD}} Gaël Njivarabesananteinaina<br>{{flagicon|MAD}} Christophe Rakotonantenaina<br>{{flagicon|MAD}} Philippe Ravelonomenjanahary<br>{{flagicon|MAD}} Razakaniaina Andriarakotobololona
|-
|Team coordinator
|{{flagicon|MAD}} [[Faneva Andriatsima]]
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|MAD}} [[Éric Rabésandratana]]

===Coaching history===
{{div col|colwidth=25em}}
*{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[Peter Schnittger]] (1978–1985)<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180803194558/https://www.fifa.com/fifa-tournaments/players-coaches/people=95836/index.html FIFA.com]</ref>
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Justin Rasoloharimahefa (1994)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Claude "Ntsoa" Ravelomanantsoa (–2001)<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180803194622/https://www.fifa.com/fifa-tournaments/players-coaches/people=184896/index.html FIFA.com]</ref>
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Vincent Randriamirado (2001)<ref>[https://web.archive.org/web/20180803194819/https://www.fifa.com/fifa-tournaments/players-coaches/people=63673/index.html FIFA.com]</ref>
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Jeremia Randriambololona (2001)
*{{flagicon|SUI}} [[Hans Heiniger]] (2002–2003)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Hervé Arsène]] (2007–2008)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Mickael Nivoson Andrianasy (2008)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} Jeremia Randriambololona (2008)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Jean-Paul Rabier]] (2010–2011)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Mosa (footballer)|Mosa]] (2011)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Frank Rajaonarisamba]] (2011–2012)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Auguste Raux]] (2012–2014)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Frank Rajaonarisamba]] (2014–2016)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Auguste Raux]] (2016–2017)
*{{flagicon|France}} [[Nicolas Dupuis]] (2017–2021)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Éric Rabésandratana]] (2021–2022)
*{{flagicon|France}} [[Nicolas Dupuis]] (2022–2023)
*{{flagicon|Madagascar}} [[Romuald Rakotondrabe]] (2023-present)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game#International football|friendly]] matches against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] and [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]] on 2 and 8 June 2026, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/FederationMalagasyFootball/posts/pfbid02QLLkr7WQfBEHCcbpHiMiQRk6Qw8xqy8GZ8CRYeiDoTvHfiSq8G7598vNyytboW88l|title=Fenêtre FIFA-JUIN 2026 - 𝐌𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡𝐬 𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐜𝐚𝐮𝐱 LA LISTE DES BAREA|publisher=[[Malagasy Football Federation]]|date=25 May 2026|language=fr}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://newsmada.com/2026/05/28/football-match-amical-en-or-madagascar-face-au-maroc-version-mondial/|title=Football – Match amical en Or: Madagascar face au Maroc version « Mondial »|publisher=Newsmada|date=25 May 2026|language=fr}}</ref>

''Caps and goals updated after the match against [[Equatorial Guinea national football team|Equatorial Guinea]] on 31 March 2026.''

{{nat fs g start}}

{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Madagascar in the last 12 months and are still eligible to represent.

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Baggio Rakotonomenjanahary|John Baggio]]|age={{birth date and age|1991|12|19|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[Sukhothai F.C.|Sukhothai]]|clubnat=THA|latest=v. {{fb|COD}}, 8 June 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
*<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.
*<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.
*<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.
*<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|31 March 2026}}<ref>{{cite web |title=Madagascar |url=https://www.national-football-teams.com/country/112/Madagascar.html |website=National Football Teams}}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Madagascar.''

===Most appearances===

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[African Nations Championship]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (1): [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]

===Regional===
* '''[[COSAFA Cup]]'''
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2015 COSAFA Cup|2015]]
* '''[[Football at the Indian Ocean Island Games|Indian Ocean Island Games]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Gold medal (3):''' [[Football at the 1990 Indian Ocean Island Games|1990]], [[Football at the 1993 Indian Ocean Island Games|1993]], [[Football at the 2023 Indian Ocean Island Games – Men's tournament|2023]]
** {{Silver2}} Silver medal (2): [[Football at the 1998 Indian Ocean Island Games|1998]], [[Football at the 2007 Indian Ocean Island Games|2007]]
* '''[[Triangulaire|Indian Ocean Games Triangulaire]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (3):''' 1955, 1958, 1963
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (7): 1947, 1948, 1949, 1951, 1952, 1953, 1956
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (3): 1950, 1954, 1957

{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total

|0||1||1||2
|-
!Total!!0!!1!!1!!2
|}
